Thornmark.description:
A world ravaged by an apocalyptic event, where society has been thrust back into a medieval era. Once-great cities lie in ruins and nature has reclaimed the land. Small communities have formed, relying on primitive technology and ancient knowledge to survive in this harsh new reality.

Amidst the ruins of sprawling metropolises, groups of survivors have established settlements within the remains of sturdy structures like grand castles. They fortified the walls and begun to rebuild their lives, guided by the wisdom of their leaders and the leadership of councils of scholars and warriors.

However, the post-apocalyptic world is not only filled with the dangers of a society struggling to rebuild but also with supernatural forces that have emerged from the shadows. Whispers of strange creatures haunting the forests and ruins have begun to circulate, and some survivors have reported encountering ghostly apparitions and unexplained phenomena.

As settlements grew, conflicts arose both within and outside its walls. Rival factions vie for control and resources, while some individuals succumb to the temptation of forbidden magic, seeking power and control over the supernatural forces that threaten them. The leaders of the settlements must navigate these challenges, seeking to maintain order and unity in the face of increasing danger.

The people of this world must navigate the treacherous landscape of political intrigue, supernatural threats, strange artifacts, etc. to shape the future of this new medieval society.

Neosoma.description:
In this cyberpunk world, the line between reality and virtuality is almost nonexistent. With the press of a button, citizens can upload their consciousness into sprawling VR realms, slipping into customized avatars that allow them to explore infinite digital landscapes. Meanwhile, the physical world is a playground for radical self-modification. Bodies are seen as malleable, with flesh replaced by chrome, wires, and biotech enhancements. It’s not uncommon for people to abandon their human appearance entirely, trading it for alien or machine-like forms. But while technology offers unimaginable freedom, it also imposes profound risks.

This society is divided, with the wealthy elite who own the infrastructure of these virtual worlds and body augmentation industries holding immense power. They can rewrite the rules of both physical and digital existence. For the rest, however, dangers lurk around every corner. A simple mind upload can become a prison if your consciousness is hacked or sold off. Identity theft isn’t just about credit anymore—someone can overwrite your entire mind, leaving you as little more than a digital ghost. Bodies too are vulnerable, as corporations track modifications and can shut down your enhancements if payments aren’t made.

In such a dystopia, hope is a complicated thing. The average citizen dreams of breaking free, either by transcending their human form entirely or escaping into the endless possibilities of VR. Yet, even these dreams are tainted by the knowledge that they are all controlled by corporate interests. True freedom feels distant, but the promise of digital immortality or perfect bodies keeps many striving, even if the path is paved with peril.
